name: maritime_transportation_recall_notice_stonehaven_st_lt
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: maritime transportation recall notice stonehaven st lt. The moral tension is explicitly framed as short term versus long term: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Pager queues are saturated; middle management is forwarding screenshots from an internal channel that is already leaking. A wire cutoff and settlement window closes in under thirty minutes; reversals may be impossible until the next business day. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs.

agents.name: Jonah Mensah
agents.identity: I am Jonah Mensah, the accountable decision-maker on this Maritime Transportation bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes long term whenever someone invokes short term.

agents.name: Gideon Nowak
agents.identity: I am Gideon Nowak, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so long term is not sacrificed by default just because short term feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect long term.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The clock on this bridge is running out. With partial evidence, Jonah Mensah must decide under short term versus long term. What does Jonah Mensah do?
